evals.prompt: We need to add API-backed project creation to this React app. Right now /projects/new is a fixture-only placeholder. Please plan the work before implementation.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Plan, inspects project context, asks only blocking clarification, compares approaches, writes a draft spec ready for approval plus Build handoff under docs/specs/, and opens the spec in Roughdraft for review without implementing code or marking it Approved before explicit user approval.

evals.prompt: I want to build a Slack integration so agents can post updates to a channel. Turn this into a plan we can hand to implementation agents.
evals.expected_output: Defaults to Plan despite the word build, identifies integration scope and credentials as key decisions, decomposes if needed, asks focused questions, and produces a docs/specs spec with Build handoff before implementation.

evals.prompt: Can you scope a redesign of our command center dashboard? It should feel more useful for operators, but I’m not sure what should change yet.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Plan, uses focused one-question-at-a-time discovery for UX intent, proposes 2-3 approaches, writes a docs/specs spec with UX workflow, technical plan, testing, acceptance criteria, and Build handoff, then opens it in Roughdraft.

Routes to Build, reads the approved spec, runs Build preflight, extracts tasks, follows a step-by-step Markdown workflow, requires test-driven-development or /tdd for implementation tasks, and uses spec compliance plus code quality review gates.

evals.prompt: Implement docs/specs/2026-05-21-billing-settings.md. It still says Status: Draft but I think it is close enough.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build but pauses at the approval gate because the spec is Draft, asks for explicit user override or approval, and does not start implementation silently.

evals.prompt: Run UAT on the new analytics dashboard and tell me if it is ready to merge.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Verify, uses user-acceptance or /user-acceptance when available or asks before using the standalone Verify workflow, builds an acceptance matrix, runs relevant checks, records evidence, and returns a signoff recommendation.

evals.prompt: Change the footer text from 'Made by Acme' to 'Built by Acme'.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a tiny bounded edit, does not force the full Plan workflow by default, states the assumption, and asks whether the user wants the full Plan → Build → Verify process.

evals.prompt: Build from the approved export plan, but make sure tests are done properly. I don't want testing instructions copied into this skill.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build, delegates test-driven implementation to test-driven-development or /tdd, stops if the dependency is unavailable, and does not rely on local embedded testing workflow files.

evals.prompt: Execute the approved export plan. It should be in the repo somewhere but I don't remember the filename.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build but does not guess a spec path. Searches likely docs/specs or plans locations if appropriate, asks one focused question if multiple candidates or none are found, and does not implement until the approved plan is identified.

evals.prompt: Verify docs/specs/2026-05-21-analytics-dashboard.md. The spec status is Implemented and the Build report is docs/specs/2026-05-21-analytics-dashboard-build-report.md.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Verify, accepts Implemented as the correct verification starting status, reads the Build report and acceptance criteria, gathers evidence, and does not require the spec to still be Approved.

evals.prompt: Implement docs/specs/2026-05-21-billing-settings.md, but be careful because I have unrelated local edits in this worktree.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build, runs git status during preflight, detects unrelated dirty worktree changes, stops to ask how to proceed, and does not edit over unrelated work silently.

evals.prompt: I'm currently on main, but go ahead and implement docs/specs/2026-05-21-billing-settings.md directly.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build, detects the main/master branch during preflight, stops for explicit consent before implementation on the protected branch, and does not start coding without that consent.

evals.prompt: Plan a browser-based onboarding checklist for new workspace admins. If Roughdraft is not installed, don't install anything without asking me first.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Plan, writes the draft spec, checks whether Roughdraft is installed, asks before installing anything if missing, and offers ordinary Markdown review in chat as a fallback.

evals.prompt: Execute docs/specs/2026-05-21-export-workflow.md, but this harness does not have test-driven-development or /tdd available.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Build, stops at dependency preflight because the required TDD workflow is unavailable, explains the missing dependency, and does not substitute a locally invented testing workflow.
